title: Develop Rental Management System (RMS) Service (Grizz - Go/gRPC)
description: Build the core Rental Management System (RMS) service, handling opportunities, projects, inventory, crew, and deliveries. This service is central to the quote-to-project workflow and operational logistics.
details: Initialize a new Go project targeting Go 1.22.2.
Define gRPC services and protobufs for `OpportunityService`, `ProjectService`, `InventoryService`, `CrewService`, and `DeliveryService`.
Generate Go code from protobuf definitions.
Implement gRPC server logic for all services.
Integrate `grpc-gateway` to expose REST endpoints as defined in the PRD.
Define `Opportunity`, `Project`, and `InventoryTransaction` data models and implement `sqlx` (or similar ORM) for PostgreSQL interaction, referencing the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap.
Implement core features: quote-to-project conversion, barcode scanning (mocked initially), crew scheduling, and delivery tracking.
Integrate with Redis for session caching, referencing the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap.
Add basic health checks for gRPC and REST endpoints.
test_strategy: Deploy the service to Kubernetes and verify it starts successfully.
Use `grpcurl` to test gRPC endpoints for `CreateOpportunity`, `GetProject`, `RecordTransaction`, etc.
Use `curl` or Postman to verify REST endpoints exposed via `grpc-gateway` are functional.
Test the full quote-to-project workflow: create opportunity, approve, convert to project.
Verify inventory transactions can be recorded and retrieved.
Confirm data persistence in PostgreSQL for all RMS entities.
decision_points: gRPC-Gateway REST Mapping: Decide on the specific REST endpoint mappings for gRPC services.
Barcode Scanning Implementation: Define the initial mocking strategy for barcode scanning and future integration plan.
Crew Scheduling Algorithm: Determine the initial complexity and approach for crew scheduling (e.g., simple availability check vs. complex optimization).
Session Caching Strategy: Define what data to cache in Redis for sessions and its invalidation strategy.
Inter-service Authentication: How will RMS authenticate calls to other services (e.g., Catalog, Finance) and vice-versa? (e.g., JWT, API keys, mTLS).

subtasks.title: Initialize Go project and define gRPC protobuf schemas
subtasks.description: Initialize a new Go project and define all necessary gRPC service and message protobufs for Opportunity, Project, Inventory, Crew, and Delivery services.
details: Run `go mod init <project-name>` targeting Go 1.22.2.
Create `.proto` files for `OpportunityService`, `ProjectService`, `InventoryService`, `CrewService`, and `DeliveryService`, including their respective message definitions.
test_strategy: Verify `go.mod` and `go.sum` are created.
Validate `.proto` files for correct syntax using `protoc --verify_only`.

subtasks.title: Generate Go code and set up base gRPC server
subtasks.description: Generate Go code from the defined protobufs and establish a basic gRPC server structure capable of serving requests.
details: Install `protoc-gen-go` and `protoc-gen-go-grpc`.
Run `protoc` commands to generate Go client and server stubs from all `.proto` files.
Implement a basic `main.go` to start a gRPC server and register placeholder service implementations.
test_strategy: Verify generated Go files exist in the expected directories.
Run the basic gRPC server and confirm it starts without errors.
Use `grpcurl` to attempt a connection to the server and verify it's reachable.

subtasks.title: Define data models and integrate with PostgreSQL
subtasks.description: Define Go structs for `Opportunity`, `Project`, and `InventoryTransaction` and integrate `sqlx` for PostgreSQL interaction, using the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap.
details: Define Go structs for `Opportunity`, `Project`, and `InventoryTransaction` with `sqlx` tags.
Set up `sqlx` connection pool using the PostgreSQL URL from the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap.
Implement basic CRUD operations for these models.
test_strategy: Write unit tests for `sqlx` connection and basic CRUD operations.
Deploy the service and verify successful connection to PostgreSQL.
Insert and retrieve sample data to confirm persistence.

subtasks.title: Implement Opportunity and Project gRPC service logic
subtasks.description: Implement the server-side logic for `OpportunityService` and `ProjectService`, including quote-to-project conversion.
details: Implement gRPC handlers for `CreateOpportunity`, `GetOpportunity`, `UpdateOpportunity`, `ConvertOpportunityToProject`.
Implement gRPC handlers for `CreateProject`, `GetProject`, `UpdateProject`.
test_strategy: Use `grpcurl` to test `CreateOpportunity`, `ConvertOpportunityToProject`, and `GetProject` endpoints.
Verify data consistency in PostgreSQL after these operations.

subtasks.title: Implement Inventory, Crew, and Delivery gRPC service logic
subtasks.description: Implement the server-side logic for `InventoryService`, `CrewService`, and `DeliveryService`, including inventory transactions, crew scheduling, and delivery tracking.
details: Implement gRPC handlers for `RecordInventoryTransaction`, `GetInventoryTransactionsByProject`.
Implement gRPC handlers for `CrewService` (e.g., `ScheduleCrew`, `GetCrewAvailability`).
Implement gRPC handlers for `DeliveryService` (e.g., `CreateDelivery`, `TrackDelivery`).
test_strategy: Use `grpcurl` to test `RecordInventoryTransaction`, `ScheduleCrew`, and `CreateDelivery` endpoints.
Verify data consistency in PostgreSQL for inventory, crew, and delivery entities.

subtasks.title: Integrate grpc-gateway, Redis caching, and health checks
subtasks.description: Integrate `grpc-gateway` to expose REST endpoints, add Redis for session caching, and implement basic health checks for gRPC and REST endpoints.
details: Integrate `grpc-gateway` to expose REST endpoints for all gRPC services.
Integrate `go-redis` client for session caching, using the Redis URL from `sigma1-infra-endpoints`.
Add `/healthz` and `/readyz` endpoints for both gRPC and REST, checking database and Redis connectivity.
test_strategy: Use `curl` to verify REST endpoints exposed by `grpc-gateway` are functional.
Verify Redis caching by setting and retrieving a session value.
Access health check endpoints and confirm they return 200 OK.

contextual_notes.deployment_environment: Kubernetes (inferred from `infrastructure_context` listing CRDs and Services).
contextual_notes.data_store_configuration: PostgreSQL and Redis connection details are expected to be provided via the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap. Ensure this ConfigMap is created and accessible in the target Kubernetes namespace.
contextual_notes.inter_service_communication_consideratio: The `cto` namespace contains several existing services (e.g., `cto-controller`, `cto-web`). The RMS service may need to interact with these. The 'Inter-service Authentication' decision point is critical for defining how this communication will be secured (e.g., JWT, API keys, mTLS).
contextual_notes.potential_future_integrations: NATS messaging (`messaging/nats`) is available in the cluster. While not a direct requirement for this task, it could be considered for future event-driven architectures or asynchronous communication patterns within the RMS or with other services.

plan.title: Development Plan: Finance Service (Rex - Rust/Axum)
plan.overview: This plan outlines the development of the Finance Service, a Rust/Axum application responsible for invoicing, payments, and financial reporting. It will integrate with the existing RMS service and utilize PostgreSQL for data persistence. The service will be deployed to Kubernetes, leveraging existing infrastructure components like External Secrets and Loki.

decision_points_resolution.resolution: The initial focus will be on recording payment events received via Stripe webhooks, rather than initiating full payment processing from the Finance service. This aligns with the task description and subtask 4005 details.

For fetching project details from the RMS service (Task 3) for quote-to-invoice conversion, a RESTful API client will be implemented. This provides a direct and synchronous method for data retrieval. While NATS is available in the infrastructure, REST is simpler for initial direct queries. Future enhancements could explore NATS for event-driven communication.

Initially, financial reports (revenue, aging, cashflow, profitability) will primarily draw data from the Finance service's own database (invoices, payments). If 'profitability' requires cost data from RMS, this will be fetched via the established RMS integration protocol (REST). Specific data aggregation logic will be defined during implementation of subtask 4004.

For communication between the Finance service and RMS, JWTs (JSON Web Tokens) will be used for authentication. These tokens will be issued and validated by a central identity provider (if available) or managed as API keys stored securely via External Secrets. Given the presence of Cilium CRDs, mTLS could be considered as a future enhancement if a service mesh is fully configured for it.

implementation_phases.description: Establish the basic project structure, define data models, and set up database connectivity.

subtasks.title: Initialize Rust project, Axum, and define finance data models
subtasks.details:
1. Create a new Rust binary project: `cargo new --bin finance-service` (targeting Rust 1.77.2).
2. Add core dependencies: `axum`, `tokio` (with `full` feature), `sqlx` (with `runtime-tokio`, `postgres`, `macros` features), `chrono` (with `serde` feature), `serde` (with `derive` feature), `dotenvy` (for local development).
3. Define Rust structs for `Invoice`, `Payment`, and `InvoiceStatus` (enum) with `serde::Serialize` and `serde::Deserialize` derives.
4. Create initial `sqlx-cli` migrations for `invoices` and `payments` tables, including fields for `id`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, and relevant finance-specific fields (e.g., `amount`, `currency`, `status`, `due_date`, `project_id`).
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Run `cargo run` to verify the basic Axum server starts without errors.
2. Configure a local PostgreSQL instance and run `sqlx migrate run` to confirm successful table creation.
3. Implement unit tests for data model serialization/deserialization and basic validation logic.

subtasks.title: Integrate PostgreSQL for data persistence
subtasks.details:
1. Configure `sqlx` to connect to PostgreSQL. The database URL will be sourced from an environment variable (e.g., `DATABASE_URL`), which will be populated via an External Secret in Kubernetes, referencing the `gitlab/gitlab-postgresql` service.
2. Implement `sqlx::PgPool` for efficient connection pooling.
3. Create a `database` module or utility functions for executing basic CRUD operations (insert, select, update, delete) against the `invoices` and `payments` tables.
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Deploy the service to a local Kubernetes environment (or use Docker Compose with PostgreSQL) and verify successful connection to the PostgreSQL instance.
2. Perform a basic database operation (e.g., insert a dummy invoice, then select it) to confirm data persistence and retrieval functionality.

implementation_phases.description: Implement the primary API endpoints for managing invoices and payments.

subtasks.title: Implement Invoice and Payment API endpoints
subtasks.details:
1. Develop Axum handlers and associated business logic for `POST /api/v1/invoices` to create new invoices.
2. Implement handlers for `GET /api/v1/invoices` (list all) and `GET /api/v1/invoices/:id` (retrieve by ID).
3. Implement handlers for `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send` and `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/paid` to manage invoice status transitions.
4. Develop handlers for `POST /api/v1/payments` (record new payment), `GET /api/v1/payments` (list all payments), and `GET /api/v1/payments/invoice/:id` (retrieve payments for a specific invoice).
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Use `curl` or a tool like Postman/Insomnia to create an invoice, then retrieve it by ID and in a list.
2. Test invoice status transitions by calling the `/send` and `/paid` endpoints and verifying the status update in the database.
3. Create a payment, link it to an existing invoice, and verify it's retrievable via both general payment listing and invoice-specific payment retrieval.

implementation_phases.description: Develop financial reporting capabilities and integrate with external services like Stripe and RMS.

subtasks.title: Implement financial reporting API endpoints
subtasks.details:
1. Implement Axum handlers for `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/revenue`, `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/aging`, `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/cashflow`, and `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/profitability`.
2. Develop SQL queries and Rust logic to aggregate data from the `invoices` and `payments` tables to generate the required reports. Consider parameters like date ranges for reports.
3. For `profitability`, define how cost data (if needed from RMS) will be integrated.
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Call each report endpoint using `curl` and verify that a structured JSON response is returned (even if initially containing mock or empty data).
2. Write integration tests to ensure reports correctly aggregate data based on various invoice and payment scenarios.

subtasks.title: Implement basic Stripe integration for payment recording
subtasks.details:
1. Integrate a Rust Stripe client library (e.g., `stripe-rs`) to interact with the Stripe API for recording payment events.
2. Implement an Axum webhook handler (e.g., `POST /api/v1/stripe/webhook`) to receive Stripe payment notifications.
3. Within the webhook handler, parse Stripe event data (e.g., `payment_intent.succeeded`) and record relevant payment details into the `payments` table, linking them to existing invoices.
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Set up a Stripe test account and configure a webhook to point to the service's webhook endpoint.
2. Simulate a Stripe payment event (e.g., create a successful payment intent) and verify that the corresponding payment is recorded in the Finance service's database and correctly linked to an invoice.

subtasks.title: Implement RMS integration for quote-to-invoice conversion
subtasks.details:
1. Develop a Rust client for the RMS service (Task 3) to fetch project details (e.g., project name, client info, quoted amount) based on a `project_id` via its REST API.
2. Implement an Axum endpoint (e.g., `POST /api/v1/invoices/from-project/:project_id`) or an internal function that:
    a. Calls the RMS client to retrieve project details.
    b. Uses the fetched data to create a new `Invoice` record in the Finance service database.
    c. Handles potential errors during RMS communication or invoice creation.
subtasks.test_strategy:
1. Create a mock project in the RMS service (or simulate its API response).
2. Call the `POST /api/v1/invoices/from-project/:project_id` endpoint with the mock project ID.
3. Verify that a new invoice is successfully created in the Finance service database with the correct project details and an initial status (e.g., 'draft').

deployment_strategy.details:
1. **Containerization**: The Rust application will be containerized using Docker, with a multi-stage build for optimized image size.
2. **Kubernetes Manifests**: Standard Kubernetes Deployment, Service, and Ingress (if public exposure is needed) manifests will be created.
3. **Secrets Management**: Database credentials, Stripe API keys, and RMS API keys will be managed using `ExternalSecrets` (referencing `ClusterSecretStores` or `SecretStores`) to inject them as environment variables into the Finance service pod.
4. **Configuration**: Non-sensitive configurations will be managed via Kubernetes ConfigMaps.
5. **GitOps**: All Kubernetes manifests will be stored in a Git repository and deployed via ArgoCD, ensuring declarative and automated deployments.

observability_strategy.logging: Logs will be emitted to standard output (stdout/stderr) from the Rust application. These logs will be collected by the Kubernetes logging agent (e.g., Promtail) and forwarded to Loki for centralized logging and querying.
observability_strategy.metrics: The service will expose Prometheus-compatible metrics (e.g., via `axum-prometheus` or similar middleware) for request duration, error rates, and other service-specific metrics. These will be scraped by Prometheus and visualized in Grafana.
observability_strategy.health_checks: Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes will be configured to ensure the service is healthy and responsive.

security_considerations:
**Secrets Management**: All sensitive information (database credentials, API keys) will be stored securely using External Secrets, preventing them from being committed to Git or exposed in plain text.
**Input Validation**: All API endpoints will implement robust input validation to prevent common vulnerabilities like SQL injection and cross-site scripting.
**Authentication/Authorization**: Inter-service communication will use JWTs (as decided) and API endpoints will enforce appropriate authorization checks.
**Dependency Scanning**: Regular scanning of Rust dependencies for known vulnerabilities will be performed using tools like `cargo audit`.
plan.overall_test_strategy:
1. **Unit Tests**: Comprehensive unit tests for data models, business logic, and utility functions.
2. **Integration Tests**: Tests covering API endpoint functionality, database interactions, and integrations with Stripe and RMS (using mocks or test environments).
3. **End-to-End Tests**: Deploy the service to a staging environment and perform end-to-end tests covering the full user flows (e.g., create project in RMS -> convert to invoice -> record payment -> generate report).
4. **Deployment Verification**: Verify successful deployment to Kubernetes, correct secret injection, and service accessibility.
5. **Performance Testing**: Basic load testing to ensure the service can handle expected traffic volumes.

task_summary.title: Develop Customer Vetting Service (Rex - Rust/Axum)
task_summary.description: Create the Customer Vetting Service to automate background research on prospects, providing a GREEN/YELLOW/RED lead score. This service supports Morgan's lead qualification workflow.
task_summary.core_functionality: Integrate with various external APIs (mocked initially) for business verification, online presence, reputation, and credit signals. Compute a weighted lead score and persist results in PostgreSQL.

infrastructure_relevance.deployment_environment: Kubernetes is the target deployment environment, indicated by the presence of numerous CRDs and services. The new 'rex' service will be deployed here.
infrastructure_relevance.database_integration: PostgreSQL is the chosen database. The service will need to connect to PostgreSQL using `sqlx`, and critically, it must reference the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap for connection details. This ConfigMap will need to be accessible to the service.
infrastructure_relevance.secret_management: Given the use of `externalsecrets.external-secrets.io` CRD and the `external-secrets/openclaw-external-secrets-webhook` service, it is highly recommended to use ExternalSecrets for managing API keys for OpenCorporates, commercial credit, and any other sensitive credentials.
infrastructure_relevance.observability: Loki is deployed (`openclaw/loki-canary`, `openclaw/loki-headless`), suggesting that the new service should integrate with Loki for centralized logging.
infrastructure_relevance.ci_cd: ArgoCD is deployed, implying a GitOps approach for deploying the service to Kubernetes. GitLab services are also present, likely for source control and CI/CD pipelines.
infrastructure_relevance.inter_service_communication: NATS (`messaging/nats`) is available, which could be considered for asynchronous communication or eventing with other services, though not explicitly required by the current subtasks.

implementation_plan.title: Initialize Rust project, Axum, and define vetting data models
implementation_plan.description: Set up a new Rust project for the Customer Vetting Service, configure Axum, and define `VettingResult` and `LeadScore` data models with `sqlx` migrations.
implementation_plan.details: 1. Run `cargo new --bin customer-vetting` targeting Rust 1.77.2.2. Add `axum`, `tokio`, `sqlx`, `serde` dependencies.3. Define Rust structs for `VettingResult` and `LeadScore`.4. Create `sqlx-cli` migrations for `vetting_results` table.
implementation_plan.test_strategy: 1. Run `cargo run` to verify basic server starts.2. Run `sqlx migrate run` against a local PostgreSQL instance and verify table creation.3. Write unit tests for data model serialization/deserialization.

implementation_plan.title: Implement customer vetting API endpoints
implementation_plan.description: Develop API endpoints for triggering the vetting pipeline and retrieving vetting results and credit signals for an organization.
implementation_plan.details: 1. Implement handlers for `POST /api/v1/vetting/run` to initiate a vetting process.2. Implement handlers for `GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id` to retrieve full vetting results.3. Implement handlers for `GET /api/v1/vetting/credit/:org_id` to retrieve specific credit signals.
implementation_plan.test_strategy: 1. Use `curl` to call `POST /api/v1/vetting/run` with sample data and verify a 202 Accepted response.2. Use `curl` to call `GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id` and `GET /api/v1/vetting/credit/:org_id` and verify structured (even if empty/mock) responses.

implementation_plan.title: Implement business verification and online presence vetting modules
implementation_plan.description: Develop the Business Verification module (mock OpenCorporates API) and Online Presence module (mock LinkedIn/website checks) as part of the vetting pipeline.
implementation_plan.details: 1. Create a mock client for OpenCorporates API to simulate business verification.2. Implement logic for mocking LinkedIn and website checks to assess online presence.
implementation_plan.test_strategy: 1. Write unit tests for the mock OpenCorporates client.2. Write unit tests for the online presence check logic, ensuring it returns expected mock data.

implementation_plan.title: Implement reputation analysis and credit signal vetting modules
implementation_plan.description: Develop the Reputation module (mock Google Reviews sentiment) and Credit Signals module (mock commercial credit API) as part of the vetting pipeline.
implementation_plan.details: 1. Implement logic for mocking Google Reviews sentiment analysis.2. Create a mock client for a commercial credit API to simulate credit signal retrieval.
implementation_plan.test_strategy: 1. Write unit tests for the mock Google Reviews sentiment analysis.2. Write unit tests for the mock commercial credit API client, ensuring it returns expected mock data.

implementation_plan.title: Implement final lead scoring, persistence, and error handling
implementation_plan.description: Implement the weighted algorithm to compute the GREEN/YELLOW/RED lead score, store vetting results in PostgreSQL, and ensure robust error handling and retry mechanisms for external API calls.
implementation_plan.details: 1. Implement the weighted algorithm to combine results from all vetting modules and compute the final `LeadScore`.2. Store the complete `VettingResult` (including `LeadScore`) in PostgreSQL using `sqlx`, referencing the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap.3. Implement error handling and retry logic for all external API calls (mocked or real).
implementation_plan.test_strategy: 1. Call `POST /api/v1/vetting/run` with various mock inputs and verify the `final_score` is correctly computed and persisted in PostgreSQL.2. Simulate API failures (e.g., network errors, 500s) and verify the service handles them gracefully with retries or appropriate error responses.

key_decision_points: External API Mocking Strategy: Define the specific mocking framework or approach for OpenCorporates, LinkedIn, Google Reviews, and commercial credit APIs.
Lead Scoring Algorithm Weights: Determine the exact weighting and thresholds for each vetting signal to compute the GREEN/YELLOW/RED score.
Data Retention Policy: Define how long vetting results are stored and any data privacy considerations.
Inter-service Authentication: How will the Vetting service authenticate calls from other services (e.g., RMS, AI Agent)? (e.g., JWT, API keys, mTLS).
next_steps: Begin with subtask 5001 to initialize the Rust project and define core data models and migrations.
Concurrently, address the 'External API Mocking Strategy' decision point to ensure a consistent approach for subtasks 5003 and 5004.
Start planning for Kubernetes deployment, including how the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap will be accessed and how `ExternalSecrets` will be used for API keys.
Define the 'Lead Scoring Algorithm Weights' early to guide the implementation of subtask 5005.

title: Deploy Morgan OpenClaw agent and configure LLM

plan.description: Verify or create the 'openclaw' Kubernetes namespace.
commands: kubectl get namespace openclaw || kubectl create namespace openclaw

plan.description: Securely manage OpenAI API key using ExternalSecrets.
plan.details: Create an ExternalSecret resource to fetch the OpenAI API key from a configured SecretStore (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Vault) and make it available as a Kubernetes Secret named 'morgan-openai-secret' in the 'openclaw' namespace. This secret will then be mounted or injected as an environment variable into the Morgan agent pod.
commands: # Example: Create an ExternalSecret (adjust source and secret store name as per environment)
name: cluster-secret-store # Reference to an existing ClusterSecretStore or SecretStore
key: openai-api-key-path # Path/name of the secret in your external secret manager
property: api_key # Optional: if the remote secret is a JSON object and 'api_key' is the field

plan.description: Prepare Kubernetes Deployment manifest for the 'morgan' OpenClaw agent.
plan.details: Define the Deployment YAML for the 'morgan' agent, specifying the container image (e.g., `5dlabs/openclaw-agent:latest`), resource requests/limits, and environment variables for LLM configuration. The `OPENAI_API_KEY` will be sourced from the Kubernetes Secret created by the ExternalSecret.
commands: image: 5dlabs/openclaw-agent:latest # Placeholder, replace with actual OpenClaw agent image
- containerPort: 8080 # Assuming the agent exposes an HTTP port for internal communication/webhooks
path: /healthz # Assuming a health endpoint for the agent
path: /readyz # Assuming a readiness endpoint for the agent

plan.description: Prepare Kubernetes Service manifest for the 'morgan' OpenClaw agent.
plan.details: Define a ClusterIP Service to expose the 'morgan' agent within the 'openclaw' namespace, allowing other services (e.g., webhooks, other OpenClaw components) to communicate with it.

plan.description: Apply the Deployment and Service manifests to the 'openclaw' namespace.
plan.expected_output: Deployment 'morgan-agent' and Service 'morgan-agent' created/updated in 'openclaw' namespace.

acceptance_criteria: The `morgan` agent pod is running in the `openclaw` namespace (e.g., `kubectl get pods -n openclaw -l app=morgan-agent`).
Agent logs show successful LLM configuration and initialization messages (e.g., `kubectl logs -n openclaw -l app=morgan-agent`).
